name: Salt Lake City (United States)

colors.symbolism: the sky and the Great Salt Lake
the salt flats and snow-capped Wasatch Mountains
the central sego lily, Utah's state flower, and the rising sun

description: A flag horizontally divided into a blue upper half and white lower half, with a golden sego lily set above stylised golden mountains and a rising sun at the centre, straddling the dividing line.
history: Salt Lake City adopted its current flag in 2020 following a public design competition, replacing the 2006 city seal flag. The new design — a stylised sego lily above mountains on a sky-and-snow field — was praised by vexillologists for its clarity and symbolism.

features.symbolism: Blue above represents the sky and the Great Salt Lake; white below represents the salt flats and snow-capped Wasatch Mountains.

features.role: Sego lily emblem with rising sun
features.symbolism: The sego lily — Utah's state flower, traditionally credited with sustaining early settlers through famine — is set above golden mountains and a rising sun, marking Salt Lake City's setting against the Wasatch Range.
